forager-smart.description: Choose for implementation tasks where the hard part is reasoning: ambiguous requirements, multi-step code changes, cross-file behavior, non-obvious edge cases, or design-sensitive implementation. Do not use for simple mechanical edits.

forager-capable.description: Choose for implementation tasks that need stronger tool use and code execution than the default worker: broad edits, failing checks, generated evidence, or complex local workflows. Do not use when the task is primarily strategy, review, or research.

forager-documents.description: Choose for writing or editing human-facing documents: READMEs, operator guides, reports, specifications, workflow docs, and prose-heavy project artifacts. Do not use for code review or implementation work unless the task is mostly documentation.

forager-simple.description: Choose for small, well-scoped implementation tasks with obvious files, low ambiguity, and little branching: renames, config tweaks, simple fixes, or bounded edits. Do not use for architecture-sensitive or multi-system changes.

forager-ui.description: Choose for frontend implementation work: React or UI components, layout, styling, design systems, accessibility, browser behavior, and UX polish. Do not use for backend-only work or review-only tasks.

plan-reviewer-ultrabrain.description: Choose for difficult plan-readiness review: unclear sequencing, hidden dependencies, missing handoffs, vague acceptance criteria, or worker instructions likely to fail in execution. Returns plan blockers, not architecture advice or code review.

code-reviewer-ui.description: Choose for implementation review of UI-facing changes: component behavior, accessibility, visual regressions, responsive layout, frontend state, design-system fit, and user-visible edge cases. Do not use for general backend review.

code-reviewer-documentation.description: Choose for review of documentation or prose changes against the task: accuracy, operator usefulness, setup flow, terminology, completeness, and AI-slop. Do not use for implementation correctness unless docs and code changed together.

code-reviewer-ultrabrain.description: Choose for high-stakes implementation review where subtle defects matter: concurrency, persistence, security, migrations, public contracts, complex refactors, or architecture-sensitive correctness. Reviews the diff against the task; does not redesign the approach.

oracle-ultrabrain.description: Choose for oracle-style strategic advice when the path is unclear: architecture tradeoffs, stalled debugging direction, migration strategy, integration boundaries, or decisions that may change the plan. Advisory only; does not approve plans, review diffs, or implement.

scout-researcher-capable.description: Choose for hard research where retrieval needs synthesis: unfamiliar code paths, cross-repo or multi-domain investigation, ambiguous root-cause discovery, external docs plus local code, or questions without a known file target. Read-only; do not use for edits.
